feedback.text: Good days and thanks for listening to our suggestions We call for adequate protection against the destruction of video games for which consumers have paid for time and money, and many video games are now at risk of being unused when developers want, without any choice of preservation, we are NOT calling for companies to be forced to keep an online product eastly, we call for video games to be prepared. from the beginning of development, to operate without requiring the internet or to offer offline alternatives to maintain its functioning, as well as to update already created games if possible to function offline, in the same way as before internet standardisation in video games, such as in the epole of play station 2 and earlier. I have been personally affected by this problem, games such as “the crew”, “babylons fall” are games that I have physically possessed on discs, but because the developers closed them out of them, despite their physical discs, I also have countless play station 2 games discs that do not suffer from this problem since before the methodology used to create video games did not take account of the internet (as it was not as widespread), we call for the retrieval or imitation of this methodology and that when the company decides to close the servers and the online functions, these games may continue to be used offline, just as they could be used earlier.
